About: It is about the final meal that Jesus shared with his apostles in Jerusalem before his crucifixion.
Affects: It affects the framework of the narrative of Jesus' life.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Eucharist.
It is associated with Holy Communion.
It is associated with The Lord's Supper.
It is associated with the institution of the Eucharist.
It is associated with the Farewell Discourse by Jesus.
It is associated with the prediction of Judas' betrayal.
It is associated with the prediction of Peter's denial.
It is associated with the washing the feet of the Apostles by Jesus.
It is associated with the New Commandment to love one another.
It is associated with the High Priestly Prayer.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the framework of the narrative of Jesus' life.
HasA: It has predictions about the impending betrayal of Jesus.
It has the foretelling of the upcoming denial of Jesus by Apostle Peter.
It has the institution of the Eucharist.
It has the washing the feet of the Apostles by Jesus.
It has the new commandment to love one another.
It has the detailed Farewell Discourse by Jesus.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by the institution of the Eucharist.
It is characterized by the washing the feet of the Apostles by Jesus.
It is characterized by the prediction of Judas' betrayal.
It is characterized by the prediction of Peter's denial.
It is characterized by the Farewell Discourse.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by 1st-century Eucharistic practice.
IsA: It is the final meal that Jesus shared with his apostles.
It is a ritual which recounted that meal.
It is the institution of the Eucharist.
KnownAs: It is known as the Eucharist.
It is known as Holy Communion.
It is known as The Lord's Supper.
It is known as the Mystical Supper.
It is known as the Secret Supper.
KnownFor: It is known for the institution of the Eucharist.
It is known for the prediction of Judas' betrayal.
It is known for the prediction of Peter's denial.
It is known for the washing of the feet of the apostles.
It is known for the new commandment to love one another.
OccurredDuring: It occurred during the week of Passover.
It occurred during the Last Supper.
OccurredIn: It occurred in the week of Passover.
It occurred in chapter 26 of the Gospel of Matthew.
It occurred in chapter 22 of the Gospel of Luke.
PartOf: It is part of the narrative of Jesus' life.
It is part of the Gospel accounts.
It is part of the New Testament.
PerformedBy: It was performed by his apostles.
It was performed by his disciples.
It was performed by Apostle Peter.
UsedAs: It is used as the scriptural basis for the Eucharist.
It is used as the institution of the Eucharist.
It is used as a ritual which recounted that meal.
It is used as the basis for the Eucharist.
It is used as a Eucharistic celebration.
UsedFor: It is used for the Eucharist.
It is used for Holy Communion.
It is used for The Lord's Supper.
It is used for Eucharistic celebration.
It is used for commemorating the final meal.
UsedIn: It is used in the Gospel accounts.
It is used in the New Testament.
It is used in the four canonical gospels.
It is used in the Synoptic Gospels.
It is used in the First Epistle to the Corinthians.
It is used in the liturgy.
It is used in the celebration of the Eucharist.
It is used in the early Christian community.
UsedTo: It is used to commemorate the final meal.
It is used to provide the scriptural basis for the Eucharist.
It is used to predict betrayal by one of the apostles.
It is used to foretell Peter's denial.
It is used to institute the Eucharist.
It is used to remember Jesus.
It is used to seal a covenant with God.
It is used to identify the traitor.
It is used to give the new commandment to love one another.
It is used to prepare disciples for Jesus' departure.
